event_signature.definition: A concrete, dated event-like unit that may support same-event matching or event cluster seed generation. Has concrete actor or product, concrete event action, and time context.
examples_positive: BENCH_1_3b_single_024: Shanghai Telecom Token plan (Chinese pricing)

thread_signature.definition: A related sequence or topical thread that should not directly seed an event cluster. Same source recurring format, same product different feature, same company different reports, or same author series.
examples_positive: BENCH_1_3b_pair_035: Codex remote control vs Windows sandbox
BENCH_1_3b_pair_044: YC Modern vs YouArt launches
examples_negative: BENCH_1_3b_pair_039: Notion CLI + Workers → event_signature (coordinated launch)
BENCH_1_3b_pair_040: OpenShell v0.0.40 + v0.0.41 → event_signature (sequential releases)

content_signature.definition: Structured content useful for search, filtering, or source profiling, but not event or thread clustering. Personal tool setups, generic commentary without concrete event, tutorials without new release.
examples_negative: BENCH_1_3b_single_031: Notion CLI third-party → event_signature (restates real launch)

reject.definition: Low-signal or non-semantic content. One-word replies, pure link-only posts, generic engagement bait, emoji-only, very short social replies.
examples_negative: BENCH_1_3b_single_024: Shanghai Telecom → event_signature (false negative in current extractor)

release.definition: A new product, major version, or downloadable artifact is made available.
negative_examples: BENCH_1_3b_single_004: DGX Spark anecdote — not a release, just personal use

feature_update.definition: An existing product or service gains new capabilities, integrations, or improvements.
positive_examples: BENCH_1_3b_single_011: Notion Custom Agents tool support
negative_examples: BENCH_1_3b_single_030: Translation tool setup — personal use, not feature update

availability.definition: A product or service becomes available in new regions, platforms, tiers, or access levels.

pricing.definition: A change to pricing, plans, or cost structure including free tiers and discounts.
negative_examples: BENCH_1_3b_single_020: Startup PUA — mentions 'money' in advice context, not pricing

benchmark.definition: A systematic evaluation of model or system performance against defined metrics.

ranking.definition: A comparative ordering of products, models, or services by some metric.

adoption_metric.definition: Quantified usage, market share, revenue, or customer count data.

case_study.definition: A detailed account of a customer or user achieving results with a product.
positive_examples: BENCH_1_3b_single_004: DGX Spark local testing (borderline case study)

integration.definition: A product or service connects with another platform, API, or tool.
positive_examples: BENCH_1_3b_single_014: QVeris CLI + Claude Code integration
negative_examples: BENCH_1_3b_pair_037: QVeris CLI vs AI roundup — shared entity 'Claude Code' is not integration overlap

partnership.definition: Two or more organizations announce a formal collaboration.

negative_examples: BENCH_1_3b_single_006: 'Concerning' — extractor labeled funding but item is about opposition campaigns, not funding

company_launch.definition: A new company is announced or launched, typically via accelerators or founder announcements.
negative_examples: BENCH_1_3b_pair_044: YC Modern vs YouArt — both are company_launch but different companies, don't cluster

research_paper.definition: A research paper, preprint, or technical report is shared or published.
negative_examples: BENCH_1_3b_cluster_candidate_049: Should NOT form event cluster — different papers

technical_blog.definition: An engineering deep-dive, implementation walkthrough, or architecture explanation tied to a specific product or system.

security.definition: A security vulnerability, fix, advisory, or incident is disclosed.
positive_examples: BENCH_1_3b_single_002: OpenShell had security fixes but primary action is release, not security

event.definition: A dated gathering: conference, webinar, meetup, summit, or livestream.
negative_examples: BENCH_1_3b_pair_047: AI Security Summit vs LangChain Interrupt — both events but different events

tutorial.definition: Instructional content showing how to use a product or technique.
negative_examples: BENCH_1_3b_single_030: Translation setup — personal share, not tutorial

opinion_analysis.definition: Subjective commentary, analysis, or opinion not tied to a concrete event.

valid_actor: Organization name: Google, NVIDIA, Notion, OpenAI, Anthropic, LangChain, GitHub, Y Combinator
Product owner when clear: PLAN0 for PLAN0 launch, QVeris for QVeris CLI
Company being launched: Recursive for Recursive launch, Adialante for Adialante launch
Event organizer: Snyk for AI Security Summit, AI Engineer for promotion
Empty string when no clear actor exists (don't guess)
invalid_actor: Former employer as actor: Meta is NOT the actor for Recursive launch (BENCH_1_3b_single_003)
Integration target as actor: Claude is NOT the actor for QVeris CLI (BENCH_1_3b_single_014)
Product as organization: Codex is a product, not an org (BENCH_1_3b_single_013)
URL fragments, version numbers, or random tokens
Social media handles unless directly tied to the event
actor_product_disambiguation: If text says 'X launched Y': X is actor, Y is product
If text says 'Y by X' or 'X's Y': X is actor, Y is product
If text says 'X for Y' (integration): X is product, Y is integration target
If text says 'former X director launches Y': X is former employer (NOT actor), Y is launched company (actor)
When in doubt, prefer empty actor over wrong actor
versioned_release_rules: Product name should include the base name + version: 'OpenShell v0.0.40'
Actor is the organization that maintains the product
Version alone is never sufficient as product

valid_product: Named product: Googlebook, Notion CLI, OpenShell, Claude Code, LangSmith Fleet
Named company being launched: Recursive, PLAN0, Adialante
Named model: Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, DGX Spark
Named event: AI Security Summit, LangChain Interrupt
Named service plan: Token calling plan, LangSmith Fleet free tier
Empty string when no product exists (opinion, philosophy, social)
invalid_product: URL fragments: fkR2wVD129, nRjIqRD2fg, U4n62, yZgH0hEATU (BENCH_1_3b_single_010 et al.)
Generic phrases: 'for updates from my phone', 'made just for developers'
Verb phrases: 'Give your Custom Agents', 'Build Video Analytics AI Agents'

accept_event_signature: Concrete actor AND concrete product AND non-other action
Versioned software release with product name and version number
Dated event with organizer, name, and location/date
Pricing/availability change with product name and specific change
Adoption/metric with actor and specific data point
Company launch with company name and launch action
accept_thread_signature: Same source recurring format (YC launches, AK paper feed)
accept_content_signature: Has concrete entities but no event action
Personal tool setup or workflow share
Generic technical commentary with named products
reject: Title < 4 words with no concrete entity
Pure link-only post with no context
Generic AI/agent/API terms without named actor or product
All three invalid reasons fire AND concreteness < 0.3
safeguards_against_1_2e_overclustering: Generic token overlap (agent, AI, API, model, research, paper) must not cause event clustering
Same source alone is insufficient for event clustering
CJK substring overlap must pass semantic validation before candidate generation
Recurring format (YC launches) must not auto-cluster
safeguards_against_1_3_overrejection: Chinese action language (发布, 推出, 上线, 套餐) must be detected
Versioned releases (v0.0.X pattern) should default to release action
Pricing keywords in any language should trigger pricing/availability path
Conference/summit/webinar names should trigger event action
Product name in title + from official source → consider release action

near_duplicate.definition: Two items report the same factual content, possibly in different languages or from different sources. One can be folded into the other.
positive_examples: BENCH_1_3b_pair_041: NVIDIA main post + link follow-up
negative_examples: BENCH_1_3b_pair_040: OpenShell v0.0.40 + v0.0.41 — NOT near_duplicate, different versions
near_duplicate.risk_notes: Cross-language duplicates may have different publication timestamps. Use content overlap, not time proximity.

related_with_new_info.definition: Same concrete event but the second item adds meaningful new fact, detail, or perspective not in the first.
positive_examples: BENCH_1_3b_pair_048: Notion Custom Agents + Agent Tools
negative_examples: BENCH_1_3b_pair_035: Codex remote vs sandbox — different events, not new info on same event
related_with_new_info.risk_notes: Requires high confidence that both items describe the same underlying event. Sequential version releases are the clearest case.

same_product_different_event.definition: Same product, company, or source but different concrete events or features.
negative_examples: BENCH_1_3b_pair_039: Notion CLI + Workers — same launch series, not different events
same_product_different_event.risk_notes: Key test: if the items were published as a coordinated launch (same timestamp, same source), prefer related_with_new_info over same_product_different_event.

same_thread.definition: Related by topic, author, company, or product ecosystem but not the same event. Thread-like connection for navigation or source profiling.
positive_examples: BENCH_1_3b_pair_045: Vercel report vs Ramp Index
negative_examples: BENCH_1_3b_pair_037: QVeris vs AI roundup — not even same thread, just false positive
same_thread.risk_notes: Do not use same_thread as a catch-all. Many candidate pairs that share a generic token are false positives (different), not threads.

different.definition: No meaningful relationship. False positive from candidate generation.
positive_examples: BENCH_1_3b_pair_047: AI Summit vs LangChain Interrupt
different.risk_notes: Most high-scoring suspicious_different pairs that share generic AI tokens are correctly different.

low_signal.definition: At least one side is too thin, template-like, or rejected for reliable relation judgment.
low_signal.risk_notes: When both items are rejected, no relation should be formed except deterministic exact-duplicate.

allowed_sources: related_with_new_info with confidence >= 0.8 and same concrete event
Same source + same timestamp/window (< 1 hour) + same concrete launch series
Valid event_signature match with matching actor + product + action
Versioned release sequence for same product
Cross-source same event with strong actor/product/action match
disallowed_sources: Generic token overlap (agent, AI, API, model, research, paper, code, cli, data)
Same source only (without concrete event overlap)
Same product family only (without same event)
CJK substring overlap without semantic validation
Recurring feed format without concrete event identity (YC launches, paper feeds)
